summary: Head-unit audio effects app crashes (native SIGSEGV) when a surround source is routed onto a stereo output graph
description: On the AAOS head unit, com.northbridge.audiofx dies with a native crash the instant a multichannel source is reconnected onto an output chain that was configured for fewer channels. Repro: start playback of a stereo track, then switch the active source to a 5.1 surround stream (or plug in the multichannel USB interface) while audio is running. The app's native render engine (libaudiofx.so) faults with signal 11 (SIGSEGV) on the real-time thread 'audiofx-render'. The tombstone points into the multichannel-to-per-channel split node: MultiToManyConverter::onProcess, reached from FlowGraphNode::pullData while the sink is pulling the graph. The per-channel loop is bounded by the live input frame width (getSamplesPerFrame) rather than the number of output ports the node was built for, so when the source reports more channels than the graph was sized for, the loop walks past the output-port array and dereferences a bogus pointer. fault addr 0x48 is consistent with a member access off a garbage port pointer. Audio drops out and the effects surface goes black. 100% reproducible on a channel-count upshift; no repro when source and output channel counts already match. Tombstone attached.
